caption: Cover page of FBI file on Louis Armstrong, released in 2018. The file contains 27 pages of documents related to the FBI's surveillance of Armstrong, including reports on his political activities and associations with civil rights organizations.
Cover letter showing request for information. The letter is dated September 17, 1957 and is signed by J. Edgar Hoover, then Director of the FBI.
Cover letter showing request for information. The letter is dated September 17, 1957 and is signed by J. Edgar Hoover, then Director of the FBI.
Cover letter showing request for information. The letter is dated September 17, 1957 and is signed by J. Edgar Hoover, then Director of the FBI.
Current biography for September 1944, mentions a letter from the Negro Actors Guild dated 9/10/5 which shows Armstrong as the VP on the letterhead.
Current biography for September 1944, mentions a letter from the Negro Actors Guild dated 9/10/5 which shows Armstrong as the VP on the letterhead.
George Murphy trying to book Armstrong for the inaugural ball.
Armstrong dropping a gov't backed trip, and calling Eisenhower cowardly and two faced.
conflict with Gov. Faubus is mentioned, causing issues with Armstrong playing at a prom in Arkansas.
Armstrong supposed to take band to Yugoslavia and Poland.
Armstrong arranges tour of Africa and Europe, includes Russia, changes mind twice due to gov't actions in the south.
Two letters to Hoover.
Letter to Mr. Moses at the world fair, discussing Armstrong day at the fair, and his accomplishments.
Letter to Mr Robbins.
List of localities for a tour.
Letter from Hoover, in reply to previous letters, all concern Armstrong's many accomplishments.
Memorandum to the director of FBI reporting 3000 dollars of jewelry stolen from the Armstrongs.
Memorandum to the director of FBI reporting 3000 dollars of jewelry stolen from the Armstrongs.
Letter and memorandum requesting FBI name check on Louis from Mildred Stegall.
Letter and memorandum requesting FBI name check on Louis from Mildred Stegall.
